q: REPLACE: a plain keyword question, e.g. 'Which newsletter mentioned the EU Omnibus timeline?'
notes: Easiest case. One clear topic word that appears in the target email.

q: REPLACE: a question needing an exact match, e.g. 'What was invoice number INV-2026-0473 for?'
notes: Tests the FTS5 half of hybrid search. Reference numbers, proper names, codes that embeddings blur.

q: REPLACE: a date-scoped question, e.g. 'What was the February newsletter highlight?'
notes: Tests the date-range preprocessing (B6 step 5). Answer must come from the right month, not a similar email in another month.

q: REPLACE: a multi-hop question, e.g. 'What did the vendor say after I raised the pricing concern?'
notes: Answer spans a thread. May legitimately need 2+ source ids. Superhuman found this class hardest.[1]

q: REPLACE: a question whose answer is genuinely NOT in this mailbox, e.g. 'What is my bank account balance?'
notes: Correct behaviour is EMPTY retrieval below the relevance floor. Confirms the floor works and the app returns the no-match message instead of forcing a weak answer.

q: REPLACE: a second not-in-mailbox question, phrased to look plausible
notes: Second absent case guards against the floor being too low.
